water.name: South Fork of the Humboldt River — Lucky Nugget Subdivision Causeway Upstream to Lee
aliases: South Fork Humboldt River (above South Fork Reservoir)
South Fork Humboldt River near Lee, NV

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — CR 25-16: Fishing Seasons and Regulations, January 1, 2026 – December 31, 2027
sources.quotedText: South Fork of the Humboldt River from the Lucky Nugget Subdivision causeway upstream to Lee: Season: Open year around. Limits — Daily: 1 trout. Possession: 2 trout. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Humboldt River, South Fork — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Above reservoir: Extends 7 miles to State Route 228; water is currently turbid with slow fishing. Game fish include bowcutt trout (rainbow x cutthroat hybrid), rainbow trout, and brown trout. Special Regulation: Artificial lures with single barbless hooks only above the Lucky Nugget causeway. Limit of 1 trout.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

parameters.note: NDOW's CR 25-16 regulation table names the upstream boundary of this reach as 'Lee' — this matches Lee, NV, an unincorporated community in Elko County at the western foothills of the Ruby Mountains where the South Fork Humboldt River's headwater tributaries converge. The printed table entry ends immediately after this word in both the scanned-image and native-text versions of the CR 25-16 PDF, so if additional qualifying text (e.g. a specific bridge or landmark name) originally followed 'Lee' it is not recoverable from the published document; this water record uses the boundary exactly as published.

regulations.humanSummary: The South Fork of the Humboldt River from the Lucky Nugget Subdivision causeway upstream to Lee is open to fishing year-round.
Trout: daily limit 1, possession limit 2, on this upper reach of the South Fork Humboldt River.

parameters.note: Reported by NDOW's own water-page description of this reach ('above the Lucky Nugget causeway'); this restriction is not spelled out in the CR 25-16 table row itself, so it is sourced primarily from the NDOW waters page rather than the commission regulation.

regulations.citation: NDOW — Humboldt River, South Fork (waters page)
regulations.humanSummary: Above the Lucky Nugget causeway, only artificial lures with a single barbless hook may be used (no bait).
regulations.verbatimText: Special Regulation: Artificial lures with single barbless hooks only above the Lucky Nugget causeway. Limit of 1 trout.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is allowed any hour of the day or night.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
